highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 updates the engine to Skia milestone m151 and ships a wave of managed-code performance improvements.
highlights_body: Key wins include zero-allocation HarfBuzz UTF-8 encoding, zero-copy SKShaper glyph spans, and managed-code colour conversions. WebAssembly gains Emscripten 5.0.6 libraries for .NET 11 WASM. Several upstream chrome/m151 bug-fix syncs are included.

bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine is now built from the m151 branch, bringing the latest upstream rendering features and fixes.

bullets.lead: Four rounds of chrome/m151 upstream bug fixes
bullets.detail: Stability and correctness patches from the Skia team's chrome/m151 branch have been merged in four incremental syncs.
Restored SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT to resolve a CPU-side rasterisation regression introduced after the m151 upgrade.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing optimised with ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads
bullets.detail: Parsing SKColor from hex strings is faster and allocation-free, and new ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads avoid string allocation at call sites.
The unnecessary unsafe modifier has been removed from GRContextOptions, making it usable in projects without unsafe code enabled.

bullets.lead: Native resource leak fixed in SKImage.FromEncodedData
bullets.detail: SKImage.FromEncodedData(SKData, SKRectI) leaked a native handle when the crop rectangle was outside the image bounds. The leak has been fixed.

bullets.lead: WASM build compatibility restored for SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM
bullets.detail: A macro redefinition that broke WASM builds in certain toolchain configurations has been removed from the Skia submodule.

bullets.lead: Broad managed-code performance improvements across hot paths
bullets.detail: SKColor-to-SKColorF conversion, SKPMColor premultiply/unpremultiply, SKFourByteTag.Parse, and HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse are all ported to managed integer math, eliminating P/Invoke round-trips on each call. HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses pooled UTF-8 encoding with no per-call allocation, and SKShaper glyph results are read via zero-copy spans.

Pre-built Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries are now included, enabling SkiaSharp to run on .NET 11 WASM with the new exnref exception-handling model. A CI test job validates WASM behaviour on preview .NET builds.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: WASM compatibility work: Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries for .NET 11 and the SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM macro fix

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151.0 line with the Skia m151 engine upgrade.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 delivered a broad performance sweep — allocation-free HarfBuzz encoding, zero-copy SKShaper spans, managed colour conversions, and an upstream m151 bug-fix sync.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-rc.1.1: Release Candidate 1 added .NET 11 WASM support via Emscripten 5.0.6, fixed the native resource leak in SKImage.FromEncodedData, resolved the CPU raster regression, and pulled in the final chrome/m151 upstream fixes.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 14.2.1 ships with this release; the Tag.Parse hot path is now allocation-free.
